release.deck: More accurate memory pressure and smoother large views
fixes: Release validation now resizes the Friends detail rail through the real browser pointer path, and the resize handle is visible and cleaned up across pointerup, cancel, blur, and unmount
Freed Desktop now uses macOS physical footprint for memory-pressure gates, so reclaimable WebKit RSS no longer blocks social scraping by itself
Freed Desktop now trims oversized WebKit caches during normal memory monitoring, before social scrape preflight has to block work
Dense Friends graphs now use deterministic large-graph layout, virtualized sidebar rows, dense Pixi layers, and capped interaction redraws so 1,600-person views mount and zoom within the frame budget
Dense Map views now suppress decorative marker chrome during movement and keep rendered marker data stable while panning
WebKit NetworkCache Records now trim during social scrape memory preflight
Align feed card regression with reader only media
Large Desktop libraries now send compact feed text snippets to the renderer and load longer reader text on demand, reducing the item payload the main WebKit renderer has to clone and retain
Freed Desktop feed cards now keep remote media previews reader-only, avoiding eager image and video loads in the main feed while preserving full reader hydration
Freed Desktop no longer sends synced reader text through every renderer item, trimming another measured 1.38 MB of raw item JSON from the local library before WebKit object expansion
Freed Desktop rebuilds compacted feed documents to cut WebKit memory pressure
Dense Friends graphs now allocate avatar sprites and masks only for avatars that are actually shown, avoiding thousands of unused Pixi display objects while zoomed out or when nodes have no avatar
followUps: Freed Desktop stabilizes Friends detail rail resizing before the replacement dev build ships
Freed Desktop caches hot-path time formatting and recovers Friends graph layout work after canvas resizes
Freed Desktop virtualizes large settings lists and exports the shared date-format helper used by dev release builds
Freed Desktop keeps WebKit memory maintenance ahead of scrapes
Freed Desktop keeps large social views responsive
Feed search now stays inside the frame budget on large libraries
Freed Desktop trims renderer memory pressure and keeps dense Map rendering fast
Monitor installed WebKit RSS after renderer projection slimming
Continue dense surface sweeps for remaining frame-budget misses
Freed Desktop routes Google Drive through native sync requests and keeps Contacts on the shared Google connection

releaseBody:
(AI Generated).

## Freed 26.5.624-dev

More accurate memory pressure and smoother large views

### Fixes

- Release validation now resizes the Friends detail rail through the real browser pointer path, and the resize handle is visible and cleaned up across pointerup, cancel, blur, and unmount
- Freed Desktop now uses macOS physical footprint for memory-pressure gates, so reclaimable WebKit RSS no longer blocks social scraping by itself
- Freed Desktop now trims oversized WebKit caches during normal memory monitoring, before social scrape preflight has to block work
- Dense Friends graphs now use deterministic large-graph layout, virtualized sidebar rows, dense Pixi layers, and capped interaction redraws so 1,600-person views mount and zoom within the frame budget
- Dense Map views now suppress decorative marker chrome during movement and keep rendered marker data stable while panning
- WebKit NetworkCache Records now trim during social scrape memory preflight
- Open reader authors in Friends
- Gate inline social replies
- Preserve recovered map location labels
- Sync and update reliability fixes
- Align feed card regression with reader only media
- Large Desktop libraries now send compact feed text snippets to the renderer and load longer reader text on demand, reducing the item payload the main WebKit renderer has to clone and retain
- Freed Desktop feed cards now keep remote media previews reader-only, avoiding eager image and video loads in the main feed while preserving full reader hydration
- Freed Desktop no longer sends synced reader text through every renderer item, trimming another measured 1.38 MB of raw item JSON from the local library before WebKit object expansion
- Freed Desktop rebuilds compacted feed documents to cut WebKit memory pressure
- Dense Friends graphs now allocate avatar sprites and masks only for avatars that are actually shown, avoiding thousands of unused Pixi display objects while zoomed out or when nodes have no avatar

### Follow-ups

- Freed Desktop stabilizes Friends detail rail resizing before the replacement dev build ships
- Freed Desktop caches hot-path time formatting and recovers Friends graph layout work after canvas resizes
- Freed Desktop virtualizes large settings lists and exports the shared date-format helper used by dev release builds
- Freed Desktop keeps WebKit memory maintenance ahead of scrapes
- Freed Desktop keeps large social views responsive
- Feed search now stays inside the frame budget on large libraries
- Recycle blocked scraper windows
- Freed Desktop trims renderer memory pressure and keeps dense Map rendering fast
- Monitor installed WebKit RSS after renderer projection slimming
- Continue dense surface sweeps for remaining frame-budget misses
- Slim desktop renderer projection
- Freed Desktop routes Google Drive through native sync requests and keeps Contacts on the shared Google connection
- Lazy load friends graph avatars
- Optimize dense map markers

### Downloads

**macOS:** `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel, signed + notarized)  
**Windows:** `.exe` (NSIS installer)  
**Linux:** `.AppImage`  

> macOS downloads are signed and notarized for normal Gatekeeper installation.
